chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Obligatory for One Upon Whom Hot Water is Poured on His Head, Causing His Hair to Fall Out

text: 5330 - رَوَى جَعْفَرُ بْنُ بَشِيرٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَجُلٌ صَبَّ مَاءً حَارّاً عَلَى رَأْسِ رَجُلٍ فَامْتَعَطَ شَعْرُهُ فَلاَ يَنْبُتُ أَبَداً قَالَ "عَلَيْهِ اَلدِّيَةُ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5330 - It is narrated from Ja'far ibn Bashir, from Hisham ibn Salim, from Sulayman ibn Khalid, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who poured hot water on another man's head, causing his hair to fall out and never grow back.
Imam (as) said: "He must pay the full blood money (diyyah)."
